name: Civilization-based spawn control
details: Hostile natural spawns are suppressed around player-built areas using live civilization score checks.
items: Natural hostile spawns are gated by civilization score in real time.
Higher civilization score means stronger local suppression.

name: Monster head totem mechanics
details: Monster heads can locally bypass civilization suppression near head zones.
items: Head zones reopen spawn access near placed heads.
This mechanic enables controlled danger inside protected areas.

name: Civilization Detector item
details: The Civilization Detector provides handheld local civilization readouts with visual and audio feedback.
items: Color output shows local protection state at a glance.
Sound feedback confirms detector state during use.

name: Civilization decay and recovery model
details: If an area is left unvisited for about 24 hours, civilization protection decays and restores as soon as players return.
items: Around 24 hours without visits triggers the decayed state.
Returning activity restores protection immediately.

name: Scalable async architecture baseline
details: Core runtime moved to asynchronous services for scoring, caching, and persistence.
items: Scan and cache paths are split into dedicated services.
Background persistence reduces main-thread pressure.

name: Zero-config default onboarding
details: Default data and weights are bundled so the mod works without manual setup.
items: Built-in defaults load automatically at startup.
Datapack overrides remain optional.

name: In-game settings GUI
details: An in-game config screen exposes core tuning options through the standard mod settings flow.
items: Key sliders and toggles are available without file editing.
Settings are grouped for quick in-game adjustments.

name: Smarter patrol presence detection
details: Settlement presence detection now includes debounce so brief fly-bys do not count as patrol activity.
items: Presence checks ignore short high-speed pass-through events.
Patrol-related refresh logic triggers only on meaningful presence.

name: Offline decay fix
details: Decay state handling is corrected for offline and unloaded intervals.
items: Decay timing remains consistent through offline periods.
Invalid decay jumps caused by load timing are removed.

name: Head influence separation fix
details: Head influence scoring paths are separated to prevent mixed contributions.
items: Head-zone and baseline scoring no longer bleed into each other.
Score composition remains deterministic per source.

name: Detector threshold color correctness
details: Detector color thresholds are corrected so visual state matches actual score bands.
items: Each color maps to its intended threshold range.
Borderline transitions no longer show wrong colors.

name: Unloaded-region score correctness
details: First-teleport score inflation is fixed by treating unloaded regions as zero instead of a maximum estimate.
items: Teleporting into unvisited space no longer briefly reports inflated civilization.
Unloaded-region reads now default to zero for stable correctness.

name: Full 1.21 series compatibility
details: The codebase is updated for 1.21 API and mapping changes.
items: Registries and hooks are aligned with the target API.
Platform glue is adjusted for mapping differences.

name: Dimension-wide head attraction
details: Head zones can attract hostile spawn pressure across the configured search scope.
items: Attraction can be evaluated beyond immediate local override.
Head influence supports broader zone targeting.

name: Fusion architecture performance revamp
details: Fusion Architecture performs a 0-1 engine rewrite with the same gameplay behavior at much higher runtime efficiency.
items: Core processing flow is rewritten for large performance gains.
Feature behavior remains consistent with pre-rewrite semantics.

name: Patrol influence range slider
details: Patrol influence radius is configurable directly from settings.
items: Range can be tuned without datapack edits.
Runtime checks use the configured radius consistently.

name: Natural-spawn-only civilization gating
details: Civilization checks now apply only to natural hostile spawns.
items: Spawn eggs, spawners, commands, raids, and zombie reinforcements bypass civilization gating.
Natural spawn sources remain the only path filtered by civilization score.

name: Detector sonar and shockwave visuals
details: Detector Sonar adds a charge-up scan with rising tone and an expanding shockwave pulse.
items: Particle colors encode local state: white for protected ground, soul-blue for exposed territory, orange for active totem zones.
The pulse sweeps terrain outward as a handheld detector scan.

name: Aura wall boundary renderer
details: Aura Walls render boundary faces during detector sonar sweeps.
items: Gold walls mark civilization edges and amethyst walls mark totem-zone edges.
Walls breathe and fade over time, and existing walls hold steady when a new scan is fired.

name: Sonar charge particle correctness
details: Sonar charge-up particles now match active totem-zone semantics.
items: Charge-up columns no longer show civilization particles inside active totem zones.
Active totem-zone charge-up now displays orange fire consistently.

name: Block scoring framework rebalance
details: Block scoring is rebalanced around crafting complexity and symbolic weight.
items: Early-workbench blocks stay low, iron-age workstations are stronger, and Nether/End anchors carry higher weight.
Beacon and conduit remain top-weight structural anchors, with glow/magical bonuses applied on top.

name: Expanded scored blocks and mod compat
details: Scored block coverage expands to new vanilla blocks and built-in mod compatibility sets.
items: New scored vanilla blocks include bells, respawn anchors, lodestones, end rods, and decorated pots.
Built-in support includes Farmers Delight, Supplementaries, and Create block sets.

name: Datapack-driven block/head registries
details: Block scoring and head registries are loaded from JSON and fully overridable through datapacks.
items: Pack authors can add or override weights and head mappings without code edits.
Registry loading supports replace-style overrides for full dataset replacement when needed.

name: Dimension-aware skull behavior
details: Head behavior supports per-dimension rule handling.
items: Dimension context is part of head influence evaluation.
Rules can differ across dimensions in the same world.

name: Wither conversion restoration
details: Wither-related conversion behavior is restored for supported head interactions.
items: Conversion paths now run where they were previously skipped.
Related head logic resolves with the expected conversion state.

name: Conversion ping-pong fix
details: Conversion gating is corrected to prevent repeated bypass loops.
items: Gate state transitions no longer bounce between conflicting paths.
Conversion flow exits cleanly after valid completion.

name: Public documentation wiki
details: Public wiki documentation is published for system behavior and configuration.
items: Core mechanics are documented for players and pack authors.
Configuration references are organized for maintenance use.

name: Mob flee AI and panic behavior
details: Hostile mobs can flee from high-civilization zones with dedicated panic behavior.
items: Flee path selection reacts to civilization pressure.
Panic mode uses distinct movement goals from normal wandering.

name: Mob flee border pathing fix
details: Border pathing for flee behavior is corrected to avoid oscillation at zone edges.
items: Edge transitions choose stable escape directions.
Mobs stop stalling at boundary lines while fleeing.

name: Head-zone entry correctness fix
details: Entry detection for head zones is corrected for consistent state changes.
items: Crossing into zone bounds now triggers reliably.
Zone entry checks no longer miss near-boundary movement.

name: Mob Flee UI cleanup
details: The empty expandable Mob Flee AI section is removed from the in-game settings UI.
items: Mob Flee AI is exposed as a single toggle in this stage.
The previous empty expandable panel is no longer shown.

name: Combat panic visual effects
details: Combat panic states gain dedicated visual feedback effects.
items: Panic transitions render recognizable cues during engagement.
Effects are tied to panic-triggered behavior windows.

name: Architectury refactor and port matrix
details: Civillis now ships a standardized port matrix across Fabric, Forge, and NeoForge for the full 1.20.1-1.21.11 line.
items: Supported ranges are explicitly aligned to 1.20.1 through 1.21.11 across the three-loader release matrix.
Architectury shared-core refactor and platform glue are used to keep cross-platform behavior consistent.

name: Detector crafting compatibility fix
details: Civil Detector crafting is restored across affected 1.20.1-1.21.1 ports.
items: Cross-version recipe compatibility no longer leaves civil_detector uncraftable on part of the port matrix.
Detector recipe resolution now behaves consistently on supported ports.

name: Head index acceleration and range cap
details: Head lookups are accelerated with indexing and bounded scan ranges.
items: Spatial queries avoid full repeated scans.
Range caps limit expensive far-field checks.

name: Mapping/remap stability fixes
details: Remap and mapping-edge failures are corrected for cross-loader build stability.
items: Problematic name-resolution cases are patched in the build path.
Packaging pipeline runs with fewer remap breakpoints.

name: Bell-on-lodestone stationary sonar
details: Placing a bell on a lodestone creates a stationary sonar that can be triggered by ringing the bell.
items: Right-clicking the bell runs a surrounding civilization sweep without requiring a Civil Detector.
This provides a vanilla-style fixed detection station before building.

name: Sonar config reorganization
details: Sonar visualization settings are reorganized into clearer detector and bell controls.
items: Detector sonar can be disabled independently in settings.
Bell sonar always runs when rung and uses a larger default range for stationary detection stations.

name: Bell sonar tuning profile
details: Bell sonar is tuned as a heavier profile than detector sonar.
items: Bell sonar uses a longer cooldown than the portable detector sonar.
Bell sonar keeps aura wall effects visible for longer than detector sonar.

name: H2 conflict isolation for modpacks
details: H2 packaging conflicts with other mods are isolated through safer relocation and loading paths.
items: Database classes are relocated to avoid shared-class collisions.
Runtime initialization handles conflicting environments more safely.

name: Mob head system toggle
details: A global toggle can disable mob-head gameplay effects while keeping head data intact.
items: Spawn attraction, detector zone display, and flee-AI head awareness can be switched off together.
Decorative head placement remains available without gameplay-side head effects.

name: Civilization decay toggle
details: A decay toggle can disable time-based civilization decay entirely.
items: When disabled, settlements do not lose freshness over time.
Decay/recovery processing respects the toggle state at runtime.

name: Config label clarity update
details: Core config labels are renamed for clearer semantics.
items: "Spawn Suppression Strength" is renamed to "Civilization Strength".
"Detection Range" is renamed to "Max. Civilization Radius" with block units shown.

name: Persistence engine migration (H2 -> NBT)
details: Persistence storage is moved from H2 to a custom NBT-based layer with a dedicated I/O queue.
items: Civilization scores, mob heads, and undying anchors now persist through the NBT storage path.
Functional behavior remains unchanged while the packaged mod size becomes much smaller.

name: Podium of Undying system
details: Podium of Undying adds an altar-based survival system in high-civilization areas.
items: The altar layout uses gold blocks, waxed oxidized cut copper stairs, and an emerald core as the build hint set.
After activation, fatal damage teleports the player to the podium and applies totem-style recovery effects.
Each altar activation is single-use until reactivated.

name: Undying config screen update
details: Undying-related settings are integrated into the in-game configuration screen.
items: The Podium of Undying section includes controls such as enable/disable, civilization requirement threshold, and cooldown-related options.
A Miscellaneous category is added for options that do not fit cleanly into other sections.

name: World-switch cache isolation
details: Caches are isolated across world switches to prevent stale state leakage.
items: World-specific caches reset on dimension or world context change.
Previous world artifacts no longer affect new world reads.

name: World-switch payload safety
details: World-switch handling is hardened for in-progress sonar, particle, and related payload flows.
items: Changing worlds during payload execution no longer leaves unsafe completion paths.
Payload processing now exits safely across world transitions.

name: Podium drain stability fix
details: Podium civilization-drain instability is fixed in far-distance and unloaded-chunk scenarios.
items: Moving far away from an activated podium no longer creates unexpected civilization hollows over time.
Low-read instability paths tied to unloaded chunks and high-mobility movement are corrected.

name: Podium shared-gold deactivation fix
details: A shared-gold podium deactivation bug is fixed.
items: When two Podiums of Undying share connected gold blocks, breaking the shared block now deactivates both podiums correctly.

name: Tag-based block weight loading fix
details: Tag-driven block weight loading is fixed for reliable registry resolution.
items: Tag entries resolve into expected weighted block sets.
Missing or misread tag weights no longer silently fail.

name: /civil rebuild recovery workflow
details: The /civil rebuild command provides a one-shot recovery pass to clear stale or historically polluted civilization data.
items: Running the command rebuilds clean civilization state for the world.
After updating to 1.2.1-release, running it once is recommended for existing worlds.

name: Prefetch-driven decay accuracy refactor
details: Prefetch is being rebuilt so civilization decay and recovery timing stays accurate from the start while runtime cost stays controlled.
items: Result-centered prefetch keeps important areas warm so decay calculations are less delayed or skewed.
Round-robin and per-tick budget control keep this accuracy upgrade from creating heavy frame-time spikes.
Storage cleanup paths are merged into this refactor to prevent unnecessary NBT growth during long-world runs.

name: Meaningful-civilization decay gating
details: Raw-epsilon gating limits decay/presence work to places that actually have meaningful civilization activity.
items: Areas without meaningful civilization no longer run pointless decay and presence updates.
Decay behavior remains focused on settlements that players actually care about.

name: Public roadmap website
details: A public roadmap website is being delivered for release-view and timeline-tracker visibility.
items: Release view presents structured version entries and linked features.
Tracker view presents scheduled features on a horizontal timeline.

name: Civilization map overlay system
details: A Civilization Map item is planned to show civilization coverage on top of vanilla terrain context.
items: Surface-aware sampling is intended to keep map output aligned with real ground-level gameplay.
Civilization tint is intended to highlight safe and weak zones without hiding familiar landmarks.
Refresh cadence is planned to be throttled and batched to keep the item practical on live worlds.
